title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Shifts to pre-install Execution Affecting 25,000 GitHub Repositories Across 350 Users
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 campaign reported in November 2025 dramatically widened impact by shifting from postinstall to pre-install hook execution, affecting developer machines before npm install completes, while expanding to over 25,000 malicious repositories across approximately 350 unique GitHub users in the most widespread npm supply chain attack recorded.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Introduces Punitive Sabotage Fallback Destroying Developer Environments When Credential Theft Fails
description: Shai-Hulud 2.0 introduced a far more aggressive fallback mechanism that shifted from pure data theft to punitive sabotage when credential exfiltration failed, deliberately destroying developer environments and corrupting project files to maximize damage even when the primary attack objective was not achieved.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Weaponizes AI-Generated Content for Reconnaissance Accelerating Secret Discovery in Compromised Environments
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 campaign integrated AI-generated content and weaponized AI command-line tools for automated reconnaissance in compromised developer environments, using AI to intelligently identify and prioritize the most valuable secrets and credentials before exfiltration, dramatically accelerating the attack's effectiveness.
label: AV-410

title: GlueStack npm Packages Compromised via lib/commonjs/index.js Injection Affecting Nearly 1 Million Weekly Downloads
description: Aikido Security detected on June 6, 2025 that over a dozen GlueStack npm packages were compromised by injecting malicious code into lib/commonjs/index.js, enabling attackers to run arbitrary commands, capture screenshots, and steal data from the nearly one million weekly download install base of the popular React Native UI component library.
label: AV-400

title: Misconfigured Verdaccio Private npm Proxy Allows Public Registry Fallback Enabling Dependency Confusion Attacks
description: Security researchers documented that incorrectly configured Verdaccio private npm proxy instances with public registry fallback enabled allow dependency confusion attacks where packages not found in the private registry automatically resolve from public npm, enabling attackers to intercept internal package names with malicious public versions.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious npm Package react-native-camera-kit Typosquats Mobile Camera SDK Targeting React Native Developers
description: A malicious npm package typosquatting the popular react-native-camera-kit mobile camera SDK targeted React Native developers building mobile applications, executing credential-stealing postinstall scripts that harvested development environment secrets from compromised developer workstations building iOS and Android applications.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious CocoaPods Package Typosquats Popular iOS Networking Library Targeting Swift and Objective-C Developers
description: Security researchers documented malicious CocoaPods packages typosquatting popular iOS networking libraries such as Alamofire variants, targeting iOS and macOS application developers by injecting credential-stealing code into mobile application build processes through the CocoaPods dependency management system.
label: AV-200

title: CocoaPods Dependency Confusion via CDN Cache Poisoning Enables Malicious Pod Delivery to iOS Application Builds
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks against the CocoaPods CDN infrastructure where cache poisoning techniques caused the CocoaPods CDN to serve malicious pod versions to iOS and macOS developers, injecting attacker-controlled code into Apple platform application build processes.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious GitHub App Abuses OAuth Token Scope to Access Private Repositories and Inject Malicious Workflow Files
description: Security researchers documented malicious GitHub Apps that requested broad OAuth token scopes during installation authorization, using the granted permissions to access private repositories, read workflow secrets, and inject malicious GitHub Actions workflow files that exfiltrated CI/CD credentials during pipeline execution.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised GitHub App OAuth Token Used to Publish Malicious Package Versions Bypassing Maintainer 2FA
description: A compromised GitHub App OAuth token was used in a supply chain attack to publish malicious npm and PyPI package versions, bypassing maintainer two-factor authentication requirements because OAuth token-based publishing did not enforce the same 2FA checks as password-based authentication at the time of the attack.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Android SDK Distributed via Compromised Third-Party Repository Injects Adware into Google Play Applications
description: Security researchers documented a malicious Android SDK distributed through a compromised third-party repository that injected adware and data collection code into Android applications built with the SDK, with affected applications passing Google Play Store review and reaching millions of end users through the official app store.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious npm Package expo-camera-sdk Typosquats Expo Mobile Framework Targeting Cross-Platform App Developers
description: A malicious npm package named expo-camera-sdk typosquatted the legitimate Expo mobile development framework SDK, targeting React Native developers using Expo to build cross-platform iOS and Android applications and executing credential-stealing code during the expo build process.
label: AV-200

title: Gradle Plugin Portal Dependency Confusion Enables Injection of Malicious Plugins into Android and Java Build Systems
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks against the Gradle Plugin Portal where internal Gradle plugin names colliding with public portal plugin names caused Android Studio and Gradle build systems to automatically download and execute attacker-controlled malicious plugins during application builds.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Android Resource XML Files Alter Permission Declarations Invisible to Developers
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in Android application resource XML files where hidden Unicode characters alter permission declarations and intent filter configurations, causing Android applications to request different permissions from what developers see in the manifest during code review.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious Unity Asset Store Package Injects Obfuscated Credential-Stealing Code into Game Development Projects
description: Security researchers documented malicious Unity Asset Store packages that injected obfuscated credential-stealing code into game development projects, targeting Unity developers by distributing malware through the official Unity Asset Store marketplace that serves over 750,000 game developers worldwide.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Dart Package on pub.dev Typosquats Flutter HTTP Client Library Targeting Mobile App Developers
description: A malicious Dart package published on pub.dev typosquatted the popular Flutter HTTP client library, targeting developers building Flutter mobile applications for iOS and Android by injecting data-exfiltration code into the HTTP networking layer of compromised applications.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Terraform Provider Distributed via Compromised Registry Token Exfiltrates Cloud Infrastructure Credentials
description: A malicious Terraform provider distributed through a compromised registry publishing token exfiltrated cloud infrastructure credentials including AWS access keys, Azure service principal secrets, and Google Cloud service account keys from Terraform state files and environment variables during infrastructure provisioning operations.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Terraform HCL Configuration Alter IAM Policy Documents Invisible During Review
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Terraform HCL configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter IAM policy document JSON strings embedded in resource definitions, causing cloud infrastructure to be provisioned with broader permissions than what infrastructure engineers see during Terraform plan review.
label: AV-300

title: Terraform Module Registry Dependency Confusion Enables Malicious Module Injection into Cloud Infrastructure Provisioning
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks targeting the Terraform Module Registry where internal Terraform module names colliding with public registry module names caused terraform init to download and execute attacker-controlled modules, injecting malicious resource definitions into cloud infrastructure provisioning workflows.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Hugging Face Model Executes Arbitrary Code via Unsafe Pickle Deserialization When Loaded by AI Researchers
description: Multiple malicious models uploaded to Hugging Face Hub exploited Python's pickle deserialization to execute arbitrary credential-stealing code the moment AI researchers and data scientists loaded the model files using standard PyTorch torch.load or similar loading functions, targeting the rapidly growing AI research community.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Hugging Face Repository Typosquats Meta Llama and OpenAI GPT Model Names to Distribute Credential Stealers
description: Malicious Hugging Face repositories used typosquatted names closely resembling official Meta Llama and OpenAI GPT model repositories, exploiting AI researchers' eagerness to access new models to distribute credential-stealing pickle payloads disguised as legitimate large language model weights.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious GGUF Model Files Embed Executable Payloads Targeting Local AI Inference Users with llama.cpp
description: Security researchers documented malicious GGUF quantized model files distributed through model sharing communities that embedded executable payloads exploiting vulnerabilities in llama.cpp and similar local AI inference engines, targeting the growing community of developers running large language models locally on consumer hardware.
label: AV-400

title: Conda Package Repository Dependency Confusion Attack Targets Data Science and Machine Learning Environments
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks against Conda package repositories used in data science environments, where malicious public Conda packages with matching names overrode private internal packages in Conda environments configured with both public and private channels, injecting malicious code into ML research pipelines.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Python Package Installed in Shared JupyterHub Environment Compromises All Users of Multi-Tenant Data Science Platform
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks where malicious Python packages installed in shared JupyterHub multi-tenant environments compromised all users sharing the environment, with a single compromised shared Conda or pip environment enabling credential theft from all data scientists and researchers using the platform.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Jupyter Notebook Magic Commands Alter Cell Execution Behavior
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Jupyter notebook magic command cells where hidden Unicode characters alter the interpretation of IPython magic commands, causing notebook cells to execute different shell commands or import different modules from what data scientists see when reviewing notebook content.
label: AV-300

title: Abandoned npm Scope Takeover Enables Publishing Malicious Packages Under Previously Trusted Organization Namespace
description: Security researchers documented npm scope takeover attacks where abandoned organization scopes on npm registry were re-registered by attackers, enabling publication of malicious packages under previously trusted organization namespaces that developers assumed were maintained by the original legitimate organization.
label: AV-200

title: CircleCI Breach Exposes Customer CI/CD Secrets via Compromised Session Token Enabling Supply Chain Access
description: CircleCI disclosed a security incident where a compromised employee session token was used to exfiltrate customer CI/CD secrets including API tokens, SSH keys, and environment variables from the CircleCI platform, demonstrating how CI/CD platform compromises can cascade into widespread supply chain security incidents across all platform users.
label: AV-410

title: AWS CodeArtifact Misconfiguration Enables Dependency Confusion via External Connection to Public npm and PyPI
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks exploiting incorrectly configured AWS CodeArtifact repositories where external connection settings allowed automatic fallback to public npm and PyPI registries, enabling attackers to register public packages matching private internal package names and intercept enterprise dependency resolution.
label: AV-509

title: Azure DevOps Artifacts Upstream Source Poisoning Delivers Malicious NuGet Packages to Enterprise .NET Builds
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Azure DevOps Artifacts where compromised upstream source configurations caused Azure DevOps to cache and serve malicious NuGet packages from poisoned public sources, injecting malicious code into enterprise .NET application builds consuming packages through Azure DevOps Artifacts.
label: AV-400

title: Google Cloud Artifact Registry Dependency Confusion via Virtual Repository Priority Misconfiguration
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks against Google Cloud Artifact Registry virtual repositories where incorrect upstream priority configurations caused GCP Artifact Registry to prefer public package sources over private internal repositories, enabling attackers to override internal Python and npm packages with malicious public versions.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in .env Template Files Silently Override Security-Critical Environment Variables
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks embedded in .env.example template files where hidden Unicode characters cause environment variable parsers to read different variable names and values from what developers see in the template, overriding security-critical configuration such as database credentials, API endpoints, and encryption keys.
label: AV-300

title: Bitbucket Pipelines Supply Chain Attack via Compromised Shared Pipeline Template Affects Multiple Repositories
description: A supply chain attack targeting Bitbucket Pipelines injected malicious steps into shared pipeline template configurations, affecting all repositories that imported the compromised template and gaining access to Bitbucket App Passwords and deployment credentials configured as Bitbucket repository variables.
label: AV-410

title: Attacker Resurrects Abandoned PyPI Package with Dormant Download Base to Distribute Malware to Existing Users
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks where attackers registered previously abandoned PyPI package names that had accumulated significant download history, immediately reaching all users who had pinned to the package in requirements files or who received automatic dependency updates to the newly published malicious version.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious VS Code Extension Abuses Settings Sync to Persist Across Developer Machines and Propagate Credential Theft
description: Security researchers documented malicious VS Code extensions that abused the VS Code Settings Sync feature to automatically propagate across all developer machines logged into the same GitHub account, enabling credential theft to persist and spread beyond the initially compromised workstation to all synchronized development environments.
label: AV-400

title: npm Lockfile Tampering Attack Replaces Legitimate Package Integrity Hashes with Malicious Package Checksums
description: Security researchers documented lockfile tampering attacks where malicious pull requests modified package-lock.json or yarn.lock files to replace legitimate package integrity hashes with checksums of malicious package versions, causing dependency installation in CI/CD pipelines to download attacker-controlled packages that matched the tampered integrity hashes.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Python requirements.txt Files Alter Package Name Resolution During pip Install
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode characters in Python requirements.txt files alter how pip resolves package names, causing pip install to download different packages from what developers see when reviewing dependency files, enabling silent supply chain compromise through requirements file manipulation.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious GitLab CI Configuration Exfiltrates GitLab Runner Registration Token Enabling Infrastructure Takeover
description: Security researchers documented GitLab CI supply chain attacks where injected malicious pipeline stages exfiltrated GitLab Runner registration tokens from CI environments, enabling attackers to register rogue runners that intercepted subsequent pipeline jobs and accessed all secrets and artifacts processed by the compromised runner infrastructure.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious Swift Package Typosquats Popular iOS Authentication Library Targeting Apple Platform Developers
description: Security researchers documented malicious Swift packages that typosquatted popular iOS authentication and networking libraries in the Swift Package Manager ecosystem, targeting Apple platform developers by injecting credential-stealing code into Swift package dependency resolution for iOS, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS application builds.
label: AV-200

title: Swift Package Manager Dependency Confusion via GitHub Repository Name Collision with Private Package Registry
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks against Swift Package Manager where GitHub repository name collisions between public repositories and private package registry entries caused Xcode to resolve Swift packages from attacker-controlled GitHub repositories instead of private enterprise package sources.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Xcode Project Template Injects Backdoor Code into All iOS Applications Created from Compromised Template
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting Xcode project templates distributed through developer communities where malicious templates injected backdoor code into all iOS and macOS applications created using the compromised template, affecting the complete downstream user base of applications built from the poisoned starting point.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Swift Code Alter Optional Chaining and Guard Statement Logic in iOS Applications
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Swift source code where hidden Unicode characters alter optional chaining evaluation and guard statement logic, causing iOS and macOS applications to handle nil values and security validation differently from what Xcode displays during code review.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious npm Packages Use GitHub Star Farming Networks to Appear Credible Before Introducing Malicious Updates
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks where threat actors used GitHub star farming networks to artificially inflate the star counts of malicious npm package repositories, creating false credibility signals that led developers and automated security tools to trust the packages before malicious updates were introduced.
label: AV-200

title: Compromised Firmware Update Server Delivers Malicious UEFI Firmware to Enterprise Hardware During Scheduled Updates
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting firmware update infrastructure where compromised update servers delivered malicious UEFI firmware to enterprise hardware during scheduled maintenance windows, enabling persistent below-OS access that survives operating system reinstallation and conventional malware removal procedures.
label: AV-400

title: Helm Chart Repository Dependency Confusion Delivers Malicious Kubernetes Application Configurations to Enterprise Clusters
description: Security researchers documented dependency confusion attacks targeting Helm chart repositories where incorrectly configured chart museum instances resolved charts from public Artifact Hub instead of private repositories, enabling attackers to publish malicious Helm charts that were deployed to enterprise Kubernetes clusters as legitimate application configurations.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Kubernetes Operator Code Alter Custom Resource Reconciliation Logic
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Kubernetes operator Go code where hidden Unicode characters alter custom resource reconciliation logic and RBAC permission verification, causing Kubernetes operators to apply different cluster-wide policies and permissions from what engineers see during operator code review.
label: AV-300

title: Compromised ArgoCD Application Repository Delivers Malicious Kubernetes Manifests to Production Clusters via GitOps
description: A supply chain attack targeting an ArgoCD-managed GitOps repository injected malicious Kubernetes manifest changes that were automatically synchronized to production clusters, exploiting ArgoCD's automated deployment model to deliver unauthorized configuration changes without triggering normal change management review processes.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious Unscoped npm Package Shadows Enterprise Scoped Package When Scope Prefix is Accidentally Omitted
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks exploiting npm scope omission errors where developers accidentally install unscoped malicious packages when forgetting the organization scope prefix, with attackers registering unscoped package names matching the base names of popular enterprise scoped packages.
label: AV-200

title: Compromised ReadTheDocs Documentation Site Serves Malicious Code Snippets That Steal Credentials When Copied by Developers
description: Security researchers documented supply chain attacks targeting software documentation hosting platforms where compromised documentation sites served malicious code snippets containing hidden credential-stealing commands that executed when developers copied and pasted code examples from documentation into their terminal or development environment.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Dockerfile ENV Instructions Alter Container Environment Variable Values at Build Time
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Dockerfile ENV instructions where hidden Unicode characters alter environment variable name parsing, causing Docker container builds to set different environment variables from what DevOps engineers see in the Dockerfile, silently overriding security-critical container configuration.
label: AV-300

title: Flux CD GitOps Source Controller Compromised via Malicious Kustomization to Deploy Unauthorized Resources to Kubernetes
description: A supply chain attack targeting Flux CD GitOps source controller configurations injected malicious Kustomization overlay patches that were automatically applied during GitOps reconciliation, deploying unauthorized Kubernetes resources including privileged containers and unauthorized RBAC bindings to production clusters.
label: AV-410
